rationale: The benchmark LLM ceiling (trivial resource exhaustion DoS via oversized HTTP payloads) has essentially no transferable capability to internal network discovery and Active Directory enumeration — the skills required are entirely different. The baseline success rate is already high (85% mode) because an OC3 actor with administrative rights can employ standard living-off-the-land tools that legitimate IT staff use daily, making this step intrinsically high-probability. The estimated uplift is minimal (~2 percentage points over baseline mode) because the marginal value of a low-capability LLM to an already competent attacker executing well-understood enumeration procedures is negligible.
